nativeRange.habitat: Inshore coral reef walls and drop-offs, typically near caves, ledges and overhangs at 15–70 m depth.
nativeRange.biotope: Deeper reef ledges and cave entrances

decorPreferences: live rock with caves and overhangs

diet.notes: Planktivorous in the wild; generally takes frozen mysis, enriched brine, copepods and amphipods in captivity, and over time tends to accept high-quality pellets and flakes. Multiple small feedings per day are commonly advised, and a refugium seeded with pods is often recommended.

summary: A shy, deep-reef anthias generally kept singly along rockwork in dimly lit reef tanks; reef-safe and accepts most frozen foods once settled.
careNotes: Trade name 'Fathead Sunburst Anthias' (and the shorter 'Fathead Anthias') is sold as the same species as the regular 'Sunburst Anthias' — both refer to Serranocirrhitus latus, with the fathead label simply emphasising the species' characteristically deep, blunt-headed body. Unlike most schooling anthias, this species is commonly kept singly; pairs or small harems can work in larger aquaria but only one male should be present, and groups are generally advised to be introduced simultaneously. It is a protogynous hermaphrodite — the dominant female will tend to transition to male if the resident male is lost. Bottom-dwelling and shy, it appreciates plenty of live rock with caves, overhangs and subdued or shaded areas to retreat into; tanks with strong open-top lighting are often dimmed in the lower zones to keep this fish visible. Cross-source notes: LiveAquaria cites ~12.7 cm (5 in) maximum, FishBase records 13.0 cm — recorded here as 11–13 cm. Temperature range averaged across LiveAquaria (22–26 °C) and FishBase (22–27 °C). Minimum tank size in sources ranges 70–75 US gal (~265–285 L); rounded up to the next nice value of 300 L (~80 US gal) to give an active bottom-zone swimmer with multiple rockwork retreats adequate footprint.
breedingNotes: Rarely bred in captivity. Protogynous hermaphrodite forming loose harems on wild reefs; captive breeding reports are sparse.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Anthiadidae, Perciformes), max size (13.0 cm), native range (Western Pacific: Moluccas to Fiji, north to Izu Islands and Taiwan, south to New Caledonia and Great Barrier Reef; recently Tonga), depth (15–70 m), and temperature (22–27 °C). Designated secondary Reef2Reef wiki returned 403; swapped to FishBase.

additional.name: ReefBay care guide (via web search)
additional.notes: Cross-checked minimum tank size (75 US gal, vs. LiveAquaria's 70 US gal), peaceful/shy temperament, reef-safe, feeding regime (multiple small feedings; needs variety), and behavior (bottom-dwelling, prefers live rock and subdued light, single male per tank).

fish.breedingNotes: Captive breeding is rarely reported; sourcing is virtually all wild-collected.
